name: World Champion BBQ Ribs
recipeInstructions:
Dry Rub:
Mix all ingredients together thoroughly.
BBQ Sauce:
Combine the ingredients in a large saucepan. Bring to a rolling boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1.5 hours, stirring every 10 minutes or so. Pour into sterilized canning jars and seal. Let stand for 2 to 6 weeks before use.
Sprinkle the dry rub liberally over the ribs. Allow to stand at room temperature until the ribs appear wet (20-30 minutes). Prepare a smoker for long, slow (230°F) indirect cooking using hickory or other hardwood chips.
Cook ribs, bone side down, for 2 hours at 230°F in the smoker using indirect heat. Turn and cook for another 2 hours. Turn again and cook for one more hour. During the last 15 minutes, baste with BBQ sauce diluted half with water (if a tomato or sugar-based sauce is used for more than 15-30 minutes, it will blacken and char). Serve ribs with warmed, undiluted sauce on the side.
Winner of the 1991 World Championship BBQ cooking contest in Memphis, TN.
